message.content:
In the web app at C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3, I need to understand how reposted posts are rendered in the feed. Specifically:

1. How does the PostCard (or equivalent feed card) detect and render a repost differently from a normal post?
2. What does a repost card look like — does it show the reposter's info, the original post embedded/quoted, the caption?
3. Look for fields like `is_repost`, `repost_source`, `repost_caption`, `repost_source_journal_id` in the card components
4. How is the original/source post data fetched and displayed within the repost card?

Search for "repost" in the PostCards component, feed components, and any related rendering logic. Read the relevant JSX/rendering code thoroughly — I need to understand the exact visual structure.

content.text: I'll help you understand how reposted posts are rendered in the web app. Let me search for repost-related code in the iskrib_v3 codebase.

input.description: Get an overview of the web app structure

content.text: The grep didn't find files with "repost" directly. Let me search more broadly and explore the directory structure to find feed-related components.

input.command: cd /c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client && find . -type f \( -name "*Post*" -o -name "*Feed*" -o -name "*Card*" \) | grep -v dist | grep -v node_modules

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report of my findings. ## Summary: How Reposted Posts are Rendered in iskrib_v3 Web App Based on my thorough analysis of the codebase, here's exactly how reposted posts are detected and rendered differently from normal posts: --- ### 1. REPOST DETECTION **Primary Detection Field:** - `is_repost` (boolean) — Set to `true` when a post is a repost **Location in Data Structure:** ```javascript journal?.is_repost === true // In PostCards.jsx line 715 journal?.is_repost === true // In ContentView.jsx line 139 ``` **Associated Fields (from API response):** ```javascript is_repost: boolean // Is this a repost? repost_caption: string // Optional caption added by the reposter repost_source: object // Full original post data (nested journal object) ``` --- ### 2. FEED CARD RENDERING (PostCards.jsx) **Detection & Classification (lines 715-728):** ```javascript const isRepost = journal?.is_repost === true; const isCanvasPost = !isRepost && journal?.post_type === 'canvas'; const repostSource = isRepost ? journal?.repost_source : null; const repostSourceParsed = repostSource ? ParseContent(repostSource.content) : null; const repostSourcePreviewText = repostSourceParsed?.slicedText || ''; ``` **CSS Class Applied (line 732):** ```javascript className={`cards${isCanvasPost ? ' is-canvas-card' : ''}${isRepost ? ' is-repost-card' : ''}`} ``` **Visual Structure of Repost Card (lines 745-809):** The repost card displays in this exact order: 1. **Repost Header Badge** (lines 745-753): ```jsx
{journal.users.name} reposted
``` - Shows: repost icon, **reposter's name** (clickable), "reposted" label - Styling: Small faint text (0.72rem) with reposter's name in bold 2. **Optional Repost Caption** (lines 789-791): ```jsx {journal.repost_caption && (

{journal.repost_caption}

)} ``` - Only renders if `repost_caption` is present - Displayed as user-written commentary 3. **Embedded Original Post Card** (lines 792-808): ```jsx {repostSource ? (
{repostSource.users?.name}

{repostSource.title}

{repostSourcePreviewText && (

{repostSourcePreviewText}

)}
) : (
This post is no longer available
)} ``` **If `repost_source` is null:** - Shows fallback message: "This post is no longer available" (line 805-807) - Styled with dashed border and faint text --- ### 3. DETAILED CSS STYLING FOR FEED CARDS **Repost Header Badge (.repost-header-badge)** — Lines 708-726: - Flexbox row with 0.35rem gap - Font: 0.72rem UI font - Color: faint text with secondary color for name - Hover effect on name: underline + accent-sage color **Repost Caption (.repost-caption-text)** — Lines 727-736: - Font: 0.88rem UI font - Color: text-heading - Preserves whitespace with `white-space: pre-wrap` - Line-height: 1.6 **Embedded Card Container (.repost-embedded-card)** — Lines 737-751: - Flexbox column with 0.4rem gap - Padding: 0.75rem - Background: var(--bg-secondary) - Border: 1px solid var(--border-card) - Border-radius: 10px - **Hover effect:** Border changes to accent-sage, subtle shadow **Embedded Author Info (.repost-embedded-author + children)** — Lines 752-768: - Avatar: 18px × 18px - Name: 0.74rem bold text - Flexbox row with 0.35rem gap **Embedded Title (.repost-embedded-title)** — Lines 769-776: - Font: 0.92rem, weight 650 - Family: "Lexend Deca" serif - Color: text-heading - Line-height: 1.35 **Embedded Preview Text (.repost-embedded-text)** — Lines 777-784: - Font: 0.8rem serif - Color: text-body - Line-height: 1.55 - Truncated by ParseContent().slicedText --- ### 4. FULL POST VIEW (ContentView.jsx) **Detection (lines 139-141):** ```javascript isRepost: journal?.is_repost === true, repostCaption: journal?.repost_caption || '', repostSource: journal?.repost_source || null, ``` **Full Repost Layout (lines 402-540):** The DetailView renders reposts with a **quote-on-top, embedded-original-below** pattern: ``` ┌─ Repost Quote Section ─────────────────────────┐ │ [↔ icon] [Reposter Name] reposted [date] │ │ │ │ Reposter's caption text (if provided) │ │ │ │ ┌─ Embedded Original Post ─────────────────┐ │ │ │ [avatar] Original Author Name [badge] │ │ │ │ [date] │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Original Post Title │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ [Canvas preview OR Content viewer] │ │ │ └───────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ [Reaction | Comment | Bookmark | Share] │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **CSS Classes in DetailView (contentviewer.css):** - `.cv-repost-quote-section`: Top 80px margin, 20px bottom margin - `.cv-repost-badge`: Flexbox, 0.82rem font, faint color - `.cv-repost-badge-name`: 600 weight, clickable to profile - `.cv-repost-caption`: 1.15rem serif, 16px top margin, preserves whitespace - `.cv-repost-embedded`: 1.25rem padding, secondary background, rounded 12px - `.cv-repost-embedded-meta`: Shows avatar (24×24), name, verified badge, date - `.cv-repost-embedded-title`: 1.5rem serif, weight 700 - `.cv-repost-embedded-content`: Full Lexical editor rendering, font 1rem serif **Handling Canvas Posts in Repost (line 468-469):** ```javascript postData.repostSource.post_type === 'canvas' ? ( ) : postData.repostSource.content ? ( ) ``` --- ### 5. DATA FLOW **Feed API Response Structure:** ```javascript { id: string, title: string, content: string, created_at: timestamp, is_repost: true, repost_caption: string, // "Check this out!" repost_source: { // Full original post object id: string, title: string, content: string, post_type: 'text' | 'canvas', canvas_doc: object, // If canvas created_at: timestamp, users: { id: string, name: string, image_url: string, badge: 'legend' | 'og' | null }, comment_count: [{count: number}], like_count: [{count: number}], bookmark_count: [{count: number}], views: number, user_reaction: string | null, reaction_count: [{count: number}] }, users: { // Reposter info id: string, name: string, image_url: string, badge: string | null }, has_liked: boolean, has_bookmarked: boolean, comment_count: [{count: number}], like_count: [{count: number}], bookmark_count: [{count: number}], user_reaction: string | null, reaction_count: [{count: number}] } ``` --- ### 6. REPOST CREATION FLOW **RepostModal.jsx** — When user clicks repost button: 1. Modal opens showing original post preview 2. User can optionally add `caption` (max 280 chars) 3. On submit, calls `useRepostMutation()` with: ```javascript { sourceJournalId: journal.id, caption: caption.trim() || '' } ``` 4. Success triggers toast: "Post was reposted" --- ### 7. KEY VISUAL DIFFERENCES: Repost vs Normal Post | Aspect | Normal Post | Repost Card | |--------|-------------|-------------| | **Top Element** | User avatar + metadata | Repost badge (icon + reposter name + "reposted") | | **Content Preview** | Direct title + text | Repost caption (optional) + embedded original card | | **Embedded Content** | N/A | Original post in bordered box with author info | | **Background** | Primary/card | Secondary background for embedded | | **Border** | Subtle | Visible 1px border on embedded card | | **Interaction** | Click to view full | Click embedded card to view original | | **Unavailable State** | Not applicable | Dashed border message if deleted | --- ### 8. IMPORTANT FILES 1. **Feed Card Rendering:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx` (lines 715-809) 2. **Detail View Rendering:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/ContentView.jsx` (lines 139-491) 3. **Feed Card Styles:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/postcards.css` (lines 708-818) 4. **Detail View Styles:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/ContentViewer/contentviewer.css` (lines 300-444) 5. **Repost Modal:** `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/RepostModal/RepostModal.jsx` --- This architecture mirrors the mobile app's repost handling, ensuring consistency across web and mobile platforms while being backward-compatible with the shared server.
